assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a terminal with cURL.
No prior account, API key, or successful API request exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a GroqCloud API key and receive a Responses API JSON result over HTTPS without installing an SDK.

first_success.outcome: POST https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses with a Bearer API key returns model output JSON on the Free tier.
first_success.observable_signal: The Responses API returns a successful JSON body with model-generated text for the quickstart prompt.

route_selection.rule: Prefer the shortest complete path that reaches an authenticated API response without requiring an SDK when cURL is documented.

candidates.name: Groq API Quickstart via cURL (Responses)
candidates.reason: Groq's quickstart documents a cURL POST to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses with Authorization: Bearer $GROQ_API_KEY alongside Python and JavaScript OpenAI-compatible clients. An SDK install is not required for the first authenticat

candidates.name: SDK-first quickstart tab
candidates.reason: SDK install is optional when official docs document an equivalent cURL call.

required_path.observable_result: The cURL Responses example is visible.

required_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to GroqCloud.
Create and copy an API key.
required_path.observable_result: The key value is available locally.

required_path.action: Export the key as GROQ_API_KEY in the terminal.
required_path.observable_result: The environment variable is set for the current shell.

required_path.action: POST a Responses API request with cURL to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses.
required_path.observable_result: The API returns a successful JSON body containing model output text.

required_path.action: Confirm the Responses API JSON includes the model-generated text.
required_path.observable_result: Model output text is present in the successful response.

prerequisites.description: A GroqCloud account that can open API Keys in the console.
An API key created in the Groq console and stored securely.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client) able to send HTTPS JSON requests. No language SDK is required.

external_gates.description: API key creation requires an authenticated GroqCloud console session.
Continuing through Login accepts the Groq Services Agreement.
A secret API key must be created and stored safely.
Free-tier accounts are subject to published Free plan rate limits.

platform_outcomes.description: POST https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses with a Bearer API key returns model output JSON on the Free tier.

excluded.reason: Official docs document an equivalent cURL call.
Required only when upgrading beyond Free-tier limits.

sources.title: Groq API Quickstart
evidence_supported: cURL POST to /openai/v1/responses with Bearer token

sources.title: Billing FAQs
evidence_supported: Free tier exists before Developer upgrade
Payment method required to upgrade to Developer tier

sources.title: Rate Limits
evidence_supported: Free plan rate limits for API usage

uncertainties.question: Whether every brand-new Free-tier account can complete a Responses call without any promotional-credit or region-specific gate beyond rate limits.
uncertainties.impact: Could add a rare billing or access gate before first success for some accounts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Billing FAQs describe Free vs Developer clearly but do not enumerate every fresh-account edge case.
